ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.
M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: How confident are you that you will be able to decrease your alcohol use in the next three months?
Notes: 1 = Not at all confident; 10 = Very confident; 999 = Not applicable; -10 = Missing

ElementDescription: How confident are you that you will be able to decrease your drug use in the next three months?
Notes: 1 = Not at all confident; 10 = Very confident; 999 = Not applicable; -10 = Missing

ElementDescription: How confident are you that you can take your PrEP medication as prescribed in the next three months?
Notes: 1 = Not at all confident; 10 = Very confident; 999 = Not applicable; -10 = Missing

ElementDescription: How confident are you that you can reduce having condomless anal sex in the next three months?
Notes: 1 = Not at all confident; 10 = Very confident; 999 = Not applicable; -10 = Missing

ElementDescription: How confident are you that you can get tested for HIV/STIs every 3-6 months?
Notes: 1 = Not at all confident; 10 = Very confident; 999 = Not applicable; -10 = Missing
